AB-K-001.statement: Knowledge of descriptive statistics, including measures of central tendency, dispersion, distribution shape, and correlation, and how to interpret each in a business context.

AB-K-002.statement: Knowledge of data visualization best practices, including chart type selection, cognitive load principles, color accessibility, and the difference between exploratory and explanatory visualization.

AB-K-003.statement: Knowledge of common analytical frameworks, including cohort analysis, funnel analysis, segmentation, time series analysis, and A/B test result interpretation.

AB-K-004.statement: Knowledge of advanced analytical methods including multivariate regression, time series forecasting, clustering, cohort analysis, and experimental design.

AB-K-005.statement: Knowledge of A/B testing and experimentation design, including sample size calculation, randomization methods, guardrail metric selection, and statistical significance interpretation.

AB-K-006.statement: Knowledge of BI platform capabilities (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, Looker, MicroStrategy) including data model design, calculated field logic, performance optimization, and security implementation.

AB-K-007.statement: Knowledge of BI governance practices, including report certification, content lifecycle management, access control, and version control for BI assets.

AB-K-008.statement: Knowledge of statistical modeling methods including regression modeling (OLS, GLM, mixed effects), survival analysis, Bayesian modeling, and simulation methods.

AB-K-009.statement: Knowledge of causal inference methods, including difference-in-differences, instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, propensity score matching, and their identification assumptions.

AB-K-010.statement: Knowledge of embedded analytics and augmented analytics concepts, including AI-assisted insight generation, natural language querying, automated anomaly detection, and their impact on self-service BI adoption.
